channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Siddaramaiah resigns as Karnataka Chief Minister amid Congress leadership transition; D.K. Shivakumar likely successor
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced his decision to step down from office on May 28, 2026, bringing an end to a major phase in his political career that spanned nearly five decades. During...

item.title: Oby Ezekwesili criticises Nigerian leaders over child welfare and insecurity on Children’s Day
item.description: Former Minister of Education, Dr Oby Ezekwesili, sharply criticised President Bola Tinubu, state governors, and the wider Nigerian political class on Children’s Day, saying dem no get moral right to c...

item.title: Bureau of Customs presents P841-M seized counterfeit goods in Valenzuela operation
item.description: The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has presented counterfeit goods valued at approximately P841 million following a major seizure operation in Valenzuela City. The presentation highlights the government agen...

item.title: Government confidentiality claim could restrict royal commission's review of counter-terrorism funding decisions before Bondi attack
item.description: A royal commission led by Commissioner Virginia Bell into the Bondi terror attack is facing potential limitations on its scope due to the Albanese Labor government's assertion that certain cabinet dec...

item.title: Field Marshal Munir vows strong response after Quetta train attack kills civilians and security personnel
item.description: A deadly explosion targeting a railway track near Chaman Phatak in Quetta resulted in the martyrdom of at least 14 people, including three Frontier Corps personnel, while several others were injured. ...

item.title: Profile of Jeffrey Epstein’s assistant Lesley Groff and scrutiny over her role and knowledge
item.description: The article examines the long career of Lesley Groff, Jeffrey Epstein’s executive assistant for nearly two decades, and explores the extent of her proximity to his personal and professional life. Draw...

item.title: BAKWATA urges free distribution of Eid sacrificial meat and warns against selling it
item.description: In Tanzania, the National Muslim Council of Tanzania (BAKWATA) has issued a strong reminder regarding the proper handling of livestock slaughtered during the Eid festival. The Senior Advisor to the Mu...

item.title: CM Maryam Nawaz successfully underwent surgery at Lahore hospital, now recovering at home
item.description: Punjab's Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz recently underwent a major surgical procedure at Sharif Medical City Hospital in Lahore, as confirmed by Punjab Senior Minister Marriyum Aurangzeb. Following the s...

item.title: Why cooking gas price rose from ₦1,300 to ₦2,000/kg in Nigeria amid festive demand
item.description: Cooking gas (LPG) prices in Nigeria recently jumped sharply from around ₦1,300 per kilogram to as high as ₦2,000 per kilogram in many areas, causing serious concern among households. The article expla...

item.title: High Court in Kagera Sentences Man to Death Over Mother’s Murder Linked to Land Dispute
item.description: The High Court of Tanzania in Kagera Region has sentenced Adolf Daudi, a resident of Nyamilanda Village in Muleba District, to death by hanging after finding him guilty of murdering his mother, Coreth...

item.title: Former Married at First Sight UK staff allege toxic production practices and poor welfare standards
item.description: Former staff members of Married at First Sight UK (MAFS UK) have accused the programme’s production team of fostering a toxic working culture that prioritised sexual relationships and dramatic conflic...

item.title: Government Deploys Child Protection and Counselling Teams After Deadly Utumishi Girls Academy Fire
item.description: The Kenyan government has appealed to the public and media houses to respect the privacy and dignity of students and families affected by the tragic dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls Academy Senior Sch...

item.title: Matthew Perry's Personal Assistant Sentenced to 41 Months in Prison for Role in Ketamine Overdose Death
item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, the live-in personal assistant to the late 'Friends' actor Matthew Perry, was sentenced to three years and five months (41 months) in federal prison for his central role in the ketami...

item.title: Scrutiny intensifies over NACC leadership and Defence-linked investigations following Paul Brereton’s resignation
item.description: The inaugural commissioner of Australia’s National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC), Paul Brereton, has unexpectedly resigned, prompting renewed scrutiny of the agency’s performance and internal cult...

item.title: Nicky Chandam’s photographic documentation of Manipuri performance arts amid conflict
item.description: The article explores the photographic practice and ongoing exhibition of Manipur-based photographer Nicky Chandam, whose work documents live performance arts across India, particularly Manipuri cultur...

item.title: Ottawa portfolio manager reflects on career lessons and the value of humility in financial advice
item.description: Benoît Poliquin, president and portfolio manager at Ottawa-based Exponent Investment Management Inc., shares personal and professional reflections on his journey in the financial services industry. Hi...

item.title: An analysis of Iran’s three-tier governance model and its approach to state resilience
item.description: The article examines Iran’s governance system, arguing that its political structure is deliberately designed to preserve sovereignty and withstand sustained external pressure, particularly from Wester...

item.title: Opinion piece argues that U.S. democracy is heavily influenced by money and lobbying power
item.description: The article is an opinion commentary that questions the integrity of democratic processes in the United States, arguing that political outcomes are heavily shaped by wealthy donors, lobbying groups, a...

item.title: Pelé recognised as the greatest World Cup player after historic three-tournament triumph
item.description: This article from The Independent argues that Pelé stands as the greatest player in World Cup history, surpassing other legends such as Diego Maradona and Lionel Messi. The central justification is hi...

item.title: Ron Baron Bets Heavily on Elon Musk’s Companies as SpaceX Nears a Possible IPO
item.description: The article focuses on Ron Baron, the 83-year-old celebrity investor whose firm has made a major bet on Elon Musk’s companies, especially SpaceX and Tesla. Baron’s largest fund has had a weak year, bu...
